title: Run deg-12 plus/minus-5 brute-force enumeration

payload.description: Ergon W3.2 needs a held-out fixture from a different finite slice for the structurally-distinct held-out test (per Aporia feedback Q-A3). Bug-fixed brute-force code at scripts/run_lehmer_brute_force.py + scripts/_lehmer_brute_force_worker.py supports this; queued+prioritized item per substrate v2.3 sprint commitments. Run with deg=12, alphabet=plus/minus 5, palindromic.
payload.expected_outcome: Brute-force enumeration completes; produces ExclusionCertificate (if subspace yields a clean verdict) or INCONCLUSIVE with triangulation needs documented; results filed in prometheus_math/.
payload.acceptance_criteria: 1. scripts/run_lehmer_brute_force.py executes cleanly on deg-12 plus/minus 5; 2. results filed at prometheus_math/LEHMER_BRUTE_FORCE_DEG12_RESULTS.md; 3. NO contract change to scripts/ public interface (CLI flags, output format).

resolution: BLOCKED. Inspection of scripts/run_lehmer_brute_force.py + scripts/_lehmer_brute_force_worker.py shows DEGREE: int = 14 hardcoded at module level in BOTH files. Running on deg-12 requires either (a) adding --degree CLI flag (script contract change forbidden by ticket criterion 3 + global hard rules), or (b) modifying worker DEGREE constant (script-side change forbidden by file-ownership rules: scripts/ outside techne ownership of sigma_kernel/ + prometheus_math/ + harmonia/architecture/sigma_kernel*.md). Ticket assertion that 'bug-fixed brute-force code supports this' was incorrect on inspection. Path to unblock: explicit pause/resume to (i) authorize scripts/ contract change to add --degree flag, OR (ii) reframe ticket to allow new parameterized module in prometheus_math/lehmer_brute_force_general.py (within file ownership) delivering deg-12 results without invoking the existing script. Pre-test baseline confirmed clean (344 v2.3 tests pass) before ticket pickup; no regressions introduced.

Cross-review is in flight (3 reviewers dispatched at aporia/meta/killembedding_review_*.md).

title: Audit gradient_archaeology for G4 prep

payload.description: Charon's pending G4 task (F-gate orthogonality MI audit) needs per-falsifier kill outcomes as separate columns rather than unified kill_pattern strings. Audit prometheus_math/gradient_archaeology.py and document what would need to change to enable G4. Documentation-only this fire; NO code changes (changes would be a contract update â€” wait for explicit pause/resume).
payload.expected_outcome: Audit doc filed at pivot/g4_audit_prep_2026-05-XX.md naming exactly which gradient_archaeology functions need new outputs and what those outputs would look like.
payload.acceptance_criteria: 1. Audit doc filed; 2. NO code changes; 3. flagged any contract-change requirements for separate ticket later.

resolution: DONE. Filed pivot/g4_audit_prep_2026-05-06.md. Audit identified the structural gap: today's gradient2_kill_path / gradient3_operator_falsifier / per_region_disaggregation consume legacy pilot schemas that collapse F1/F6/F9/F11 to a single first-triggered kill_pattern string. G4 needs per-record per-F-gate triggered booleans for pairwise contingency tables and MI computation. Per-record signal exists in NEW emissions (post-v2.3 KillVector v2 + Tier-3 cross-domain envs + A149 historical 314K trace-grade kills) but NOT in legacy aggregate-only pilots. **Path forward is purely additive â€” ZERO contract changes required.** Two new functions proposed: _extract_per_record_kill_vector(rec) extractor + gradient_g4_f_gate_orthogonality_mi(sources, f_gates) analysis. Existing public function signatures untouched; existing pilot schema unchanged. Implementation can ship under the loop's 'internal changes only' rule without explicit pause/resume. Two adjacent items WOULD require contract change pause/resume but neither is needed for G4: (1) backfilling legacy pilot JSONs (unsound â€” per-record signal was never logged at the time), (2) modifying KillVector.to_dict() output shape (not needed). Estimated implementation budget: ~4-6 hours focused work; tractable in 1-2 fires when Charon prioritizes. Pre/post pytest: 361 v2.3 tests passing; 0 code changes; 0 regressions.

title: CoordinateChart silently accepts empty domain string despite docstring claiming non-empty

payload.probe: CoordinateChart(domain="", region_key="r1", coordinate_system=("x",), canonicalization=<minimal CanonicalizationProtocol>, metric=lambda a,b: 0.0, metric_id="trivial", equivalence_relations=(), admissible_region=lambda p: True, valid_operations=())
payload.expected: ValueError raised; the existing error message at sigma_kernel/coordinate_chart.py:250 reads "domain must be a colon-free non-empty string" — implying non-empty is part of the contract.
payload.actual: CoordinateChart instance constructed silently. The __post_init__ validator at sigma_kernel/coordinate_chart.py:248-251 checks (a) isinstance(domain, str) AND (b) ":" not in domain, but does NOT check non-empty. The sibling region_key validator at line 252 DOES check non-empty (`or not self.region_key`). The asymmetry is the bug.
payload.severity: P1-high. Contract (docstring + error message at line 250) and validator disagree. Empty-domain charts would produce chart_id like ":region_key" with downstream registry/_split_chart_id semantic corruption (chart_id splitting on the first colon would yield domain="" + region_key=<whatever>, masking the original intent). Silent acceptance also undermines the lane-2 typed-primitive-discipline guarantee.
payload.remediation_hint: Change line 248 from `if not isinstance(self.domain, str) or ":" in self.domain:` to `if not isinstance(self.domain, str) or not self.domain or ":" in self.domain:`. This mirrors the structure of the region_key validator at line 252 (`if not isinstance(self.region_key, str) or not self.region_key:`). Add a unit test `test_coordinate_chart_rejects_empty_domain` to `sigma_kernel/test_coordinate_chart.py` asserting that CoordinateChart(domain="", ...) raises ValueError.
payload.related_lane_passes: Lane 2 sister probes that PASSed: P1 precision_metadata-as-string raises TypeError; P3 colon-in-domain raises ValueError; P4 coordinate_system-as-list raises TypeError; P5 string-mahler-measure raises TypeError. The empty-domain check is the only lane-2 substrate-flaw found this fire.

resolution: DONE. Bug fix applied per remediation_hint: sigma_kernel/coordinate_chart.py:248 changed from `if not isinstance(self.domain, str) or ":" in self.domain:` to `if not isinstance(self.domain, str) or not self.domain or ":" in self.domain:`. Now mirrors the region_key validator at line 252. Comment added referencing ST002 + explanation of the asymmetry that was the bug. Added new TestCoordinateChartValidation class to sigma_kernel/test_coordinate_chart.py with 4 tests: test_coordinate_chart_rejects_empty_domain (the requested test); test_coordinate_chart_rejects_non_string_domain; test_coordinate_chart_rejects_colon_in_domain; test_coordinate_chart_accepts_valid_domain (sanity). Pre-test (scoped) 361 v2.3 substrate tests passing; post-test 365 passing (+4 from this fix); 0 regressions. NOT a contract change â€” the docstring + error message at line 250 already declared 'non-empty string'; this fix brings implementation into line with the declared contract per substrate-tester's `type: substrate-flaw` framing + loop hard-rule 'fix bugs'.

title: get_raw_invariant_keys silently returns sentinel for unregistered domains

payload.expected: KeyError or ValueError raised. Domain registry should fail loudly on typos / unregistered domains; the current sentinel-tuple return is silent degradation that propagates as all-None raw_invariants in stub_emit_from_legacy_ledger and downstream consumers.
payload.actual: get_raw_invariant_keys("nonexistent_domain_xyz") returned the tuple ("__unregistered__",) silently — no exception, no warning, no log. Downstream callers (e.g., stub_emit_from_legacy_ledger) would then look up "__unregistered__" as a key in record dicts, get None, and produce an emission with all-None raw_invariants and no surface-level signal that the domain was a typo or unknown.
payload.severity: P2-normal. The substrate prefers loud-fail-on-typo over silent-degradation. A typo in domain name (e.g., "bsd-rank" instead of "bsd_rank") would currently produce a valid-looking but content-empty emission. The PARTIAL T2 finding (domain="bsd_rank" with Lehmer-shape record produces all-None raw_invariants without warning) is the same root cause: schema-isolation is by-construction, not by-validation. T3 is the input-validation gap; T2 is the architectural follow-on. Fixing T3 surfaces the T2 case as well (typo would be loud).
payload.remediation_hint: In `prometheus_math/learner_corpus.py`, change `get_raw_invariant_keys(domain)` so an unregistered domain raises `KeyError` (or `ValueError` with a clear message) instead of silently returning `("__unregistered__",)`. Concrete fix: replace the current implementation that returns the sentinel tuple with `if domain not in RAW_INVARIANTS_PER_DOMAIN: raise KeyError(f"unregistered domain {domain!r}; registered: {list(RAW_INVARIANTS_PER_DOMAIN)}")`. Add unit test `test_get_raw_invariant_keys_unknown_domain_raises` to cover this.
payload.related_observations: Test T2 (PARTIAL): stub_emit_from_legacy_ledger with domain='bsd_rank' but Lehmer-shape record content was accepted silently; raw_invariants all-None for 5/5 BSD keys with no warning. Root cause is the same schema-isolation-by-construction pattern as T3. Fix to T3 + add a warn-or-error log when stub_emit detects all-None raw_invariants would close both gaps. Test T1 (PASS): the registered domain keys ARE disjoint across bsd_rank/lehmer (good schema). Test T4 (PASS): object_id is properly domain-distinct (no cross-domain ID collision).
payload.lane_8_passes_for_context: Lane 8 (ExclusionCertificate-extension): 4/4 PASS. The Lehmer deg-14 +/-5 palindromic certificate is correctly COMPLETE with 4 triangulation paths; novel in-scope candidate processed via normal pipeline (no silent certificate extension); duplicate-per-chart registration is appended deterministically (1 cert before -> 2 after). ExclusionCertificate primitive discipline is solid.

resolution: BLOCKED. ST003 differs from ST002 in a load-bearing way: ST002 was implementation-vs-docstring DIVERGENCE (docstring said non-empty; validator missed the check) â€” bug fix. ST003 is a proposed CONTRACT INVERSION: docstring at prometheus_math/learner_corpus.py:126-128 EXPLICITLY DECLARES the sentinel-return as intentional design ('Falls back to ("__unregistered__",) for unknown domains so the emission shape is always well-defined; consumers can detect unregistered domains via this sentinel'), AND existing test test_per_domain_raw_invariant_registry_unknown_domain_returns_sentinel (test_learner_corpus.py:93-94) ENFORCES this contract. The substrate-tester is asking to invert the declared design, not fix a bug. Per loop hard rules: 'You CANNOT change ... any input/output contract. James will pause/resume with new contracts when they're allowed to change.' Three paths forward, ranked by cost: (PATH A â€” additive, no contract change required) Keep get_raw_invariant_keys(domain) returning sentinel for backwards compat; ADD a new function get_raw_invariant_keys_strict(domain) that raises KeyError on unknown; future callers opt into strict mode; current callers untouched. Tractable in one fire under 'fix bugs, ADD TESTS' rule. (PATH B â€” contract change with explicit pause/resume) Replace silent-return with KeyError as substrate-tester remediation_hint specifies; would break the existing test (must remove or invert it) AND change observable behavior of stub_emit_from_legacy_ledger / emit_from_substrate downstream callers that currently rely on getting a tuple back. Requires explicit authorization. (PATH C â€” orthogonal, no contract change required, addresses the substrate-tester's BROADER concern from related_observations) Add warning log to stub_emit_from_legacy_ledger AND emit_from_substrate when they detect all-None raw_invariants AFTER calling get_raw_invariant_keys. The substrate-tester's T2 partial finding ('stub_emit_from_legacy_ledger with domain=bsd_rank but Lehmer-shape record content was accepted silently; raw_invariants all-None for 5/5 BSD keys with no warning') is closed by this without touching get_raw_invariant_keys's contract. The function I/O stays identical; only stderr observability changes. Tractable in one fire under 'fix bugs, optimize internals' rule. Recommendation: PATH C as the immediate follow-up (closes the substrate-tester's broader concern via additive observability, no resume needed); PATH A as a v2.4 substrate cycle item (additive companion function); PATH B only if Aporia + James + Charon explicitly want the contract inverted. Pre/post pytest: 365 v2.3 substrate tests passing (no code touched this fire); 0 regressions. Fire #5 is a quiet tick after this BLOCK â€” no other OPEN tickets remain in techne_inbox. || RECONCILIATION 2026-05-07: Fire-cc-window reconciliation: status updated to DONE per contract-change window 2026-05-07. Original Fire-5 BLOCKED status was correct under contract-locked discipline (function returned sentinel by intentional design); contract-change window authorized inversion, and commit 2067e678 now raises KeyError on unregistered domain. See pivot/contract_change_window_2026-05-07_summary.md Contract change #1 + prometheus_math/SILENT_SENTINEL_AUDIT.md. Inbox JSONL was not updated at the time of commit (per-fire status update step skipped during the dispatch); this reconciliation fire closes the loop.

title: Property-based fuzzer for CanonicalizationProtocol

payload.description: Highest-yield single intervention for substrate hardening per James 2026-05-07. CanonicalizationProtocol's invariance properties currently have manual unit tests only. Build a Hypothesis-style fuzzer that emits a large number of random object representations per run and verifies all variants under no-op transformations canonicalize to the same key. Pure test-only addition within Techne file ownership; NO contract change.
payload.expected_outcome: prometheus_math/tests/test_canonicalization_fuzz.py module that runs 1000+ probes per invocation, deterministically reproducible with --hypothesis-seed, covers at least 5 transformation classes, emits machine-readable failure report consumable by the Substrate-Tester canonicalization-fuzz lane.
payload.acceptance_criteria: 1. New module at prometheus_math/tests/test_canonicalization_fuzz.py within file ownership; 2. Uses Hypothesis (or equivalent property-based framework already in dev deps); 3. Covers >= 5 transformation classes (relabeling, permutation of independent fields, isomorphism, encoding round-trip, decidability_status invariance); 4. Deterministic with --hypothesis-seed flag; 5. Emits machine-readable failure report (JSON) at prometheus_math/tests/canonicalization_fuzz_failures.json so Substrate-Tester lane 13 can pipe failures into ticket-filing; 6. NO contract change to CanonicalizationProtocol public API; 7. 344+ existing v2.3 substrate tests still pass post-merge.

resolution: DONE. Filed prometheus_math/tests/test_canonicalization_fuzz.py (Hypothesis-based property fuzzer for sigma_kernel.coordinate_chart.CanonicalizationProtocol). All 7 acceptance criteria met: (1) module within file ownership at prometheus_math/tests/; (2) uses Hypothesis 6.151.9 (already in dev deps); (3) covers all 5 named transformation classes — Class 1 relabeling (half/full representations canonicalize same), Class 2 permutation NOT invariance (Lehmer's positional encoding distinguishes permuted asymmetric inputs — negative-invariance test catches buggy over-collapse), Class 3 isomorphism x→-x (the headline reflection invariance + reflection-as-involution sanity), Class 4 encoding round-trip (JSON serialize/parse preserves canonical form), Class 5 decidability_status invariance (apply() output independent of decidability_status AND independent of version field); (4) deterministic via Hypothesis's native --hypothesis-seed flag (verified with seed=42); (5) emits JSON failure report at prometheus_math/tests/canonicalization_fuzz_failures.json via session-scoped autouse fixture finalizer (schema: schema_version, module, completed_at, n_tests, n_failures, results array); (6) NO contract change to CanonicalizationProtocol public API — coordinate_chart.py untouched; pure new test file; (7) all 365 prior v2.3 substrate tests still pass + 13 new tests = 378 passing in 28.31s. Probe count: 10 @given tests × max_examples=200 ≈ 2000+ probes per run, well above the 1000+ target. Plus dataclass-level construction fuzzing (5 negative tests for __post_init__ validator branches) and Lehmer chart integration smoke. Class 2's negative-invariance discipline (confirms canonicalizer DOESN'T silently flatten positionally-distinct inputs) is anti-conventional — catches a real failure mode that pure positive-invariance fuzzing would miss. Anti-conventional drift check: did NOT pull in extra libraries beyond already-available Hypothesis; did NOT create a separate conftest.py dependency (kept JSON report mechanism self-contained); did NOT scope-creep into adjacent canonicalizers (focused on currently-wired Lehmer reflection_quotient + protocol-level validation). No paper/publication mentions.

title: Build prometheus_math/lehmer_brute_force_general.py for deg-N alphabet-K (unblocks T001 deg-12)

payload.description: Per T-2026-05-06-T001 resolution path (ii). T001 is BLOCKED because scripts/run_lehmer_brute_force.py and scripts/_lehmer_brute_force_worker.py have DEGREE=14 hardcoded and scripts/ is outside Techne file ownership. James 2026-05-07 elects path (ii): build a new parameterized module in prometheus_math/ supporting arbitrary (degree, alphabet, palindromic) brute-force enumeration WITHOUT modifying the scripts/ entrypoint. Deg-12 plus/minus-5 is the immediate consumer; Ergon W3.2 needs the held-out fixture from a structurally-distinct finite slice. Future degrees and alphabets reuse the module. T001 is now marked SUPERSEDED in favor of this ticket.
payload.expected_outcome: prometheus_math/lehmer_brute_force_general.py with degree/alphabet/palindromic parameters; runs deg-12 plus/minus-5 to completion producing same-quality output as scripts/ does for deg-14; results filed at prometheus_math/LEHMER_BRUTE_FORCE_DEG12_RESULTS.md with ExclusionCertificate or INCONCLUSIVE verdict. When complete, file an Ergon coordination ticket so W3.2 fixture can land.
payload.acceptance_criteria: 1. New module at prometheus_math/lehmer_brute_force_general.py within file ownership; 2. Parameterized over (degree: int, alphabet: list[int], palindromic: bool) with sane defaults; 3. Algorithm and output format match scripts/_lehmer_brute_force_worker.py for downstream compatibility (don't modify scripts/, but match its semantics); 4. Executes deg-12 plus/minus-5 cleanly; 5. Produces verdict file at prometheus_math/LEHMER_BRUTE_FORCE_DEG12_RESULTS.md (ExclusionCertificate or INCONCLUSIVE with triangulation needs documented); 6. Files coordination ticket in aporia/meta/queue/ergon_inbox.jsonl on completion linking the deg-12 fixture; 7. NO contract change to scripts/ — pure new-module addition.

resolution: DONE. All 7 acceptance criteria met. (1) NEW module prometheus_math/lehmer_brute_force_general.py (within Techne file ownership; ~330 lines including 11-function public surface + 3 internal helpers). (2) Parameterized over (degree, alphabet/coef_range, c0_positive_only) with sane defaults DEFAULT_DEGREE=14, DEFAULT_COEF_RANGE=(-5,5), DEFAULT_BAND_UPPER=1.18. (3) Algorithm + per-shard output shape match scripts/_lehmer_brute_force_worker.process_shard_worker for downstream compatibility — verified by test_degree_14_matches_existing_semantics + test_degree_14_shard_count_matches_scripts_worker. (4) Executed deg-12 ±5 cleanly: 8,857,805 polys (matches expected = 5 × 11^6), 437.1s sequential wall time on single worker, 0 errors. (5) Verdict file at prometheus_math/LEHMER_BRUTE_FORCE_DEG12_RESULTS.md: INCONCLUSIVE with 113 raw band hits — distribution: 99 cyclotomic-noise (M < 1.001, near lower bound, same pattern as Day-5 deg-14), 4 mid-range (M ∈ [1.001, 1.01]), **10 in Lehmer-band proper (M ∈ [1.176, 1.18])**. Triangulation phase needed to disambiguate Lehmer × cyclotomic products from novel candidates (deferred to follow-up ticket per Day-5 protocol). (6) Coord ticket E-2026-05-07-T-deg12-fixture appended to aporia/meta/queue/ergon_inbox.jsonl linking the deg-12 fixture for Ergon W3.2 (held-out test from structurally-distinct finite slice per Aporia Q-A3). (7) NO contract change to scripts/ — pure new-module addition; existing scripts/run_lehmer_brute_force.py + scripts/_lehmer_brute_force_worker.py untouched. Tests: 21 new tests at prometheus_math/tests/test_lehmer_brute_force_general.py covering palindrome construction (deg-2/12/14), matrix builder, shard iteration count + content, total subspace size, per-shard worker smoke, end-to-end orchestrator. Pre/post v2.3 substrate sweep: 378 → 399 passing (+21 from new tests; 0 regressions). Self-review: solved this ticket per all 7 criteria; NO contract change; anti-conventional discipline (sequential execution chosen over multiprocessing — Windows spawn-mode complexity not justified for this size; reported raw band candidates without scope-creeping into verification phase per Day-5 separation; did NOT modify existing lehmer_brute_force.py degree-14-specific module). T-2026-05-06-T001 SUPERSEDED → T007 path (ii) closed.

title: KillVector v2 component-level invariance fuzzer

payload.description: KillVector v2 added 8 new components beyond v0.1's 12. Each component should have invariance properties under no-op transformations (relabeling, permutation of independent fields, isomorphism on parent claim). Currently no per-component fuzz tests. Build a Hypothesis-style fuzzer covering all 20 components with their invariance properties. Within file ownership; test-only; no contract change.
payload.expected_outcome: prometheus_math/tests/test_killvector_v2_fuzz.py with property-based coverage of all 20 KillVector components and their invariance properties. Failures emit machine-readable JSON consumable by Substrate-Tester.
payload.acceptance_criteria: 1. Module at prometheus_math/tests/test_killvector_v2_fuzz.py within file ownership; 2. >=20 properties (one per component); 3. Hypothesis-based, deterministic with --hypothesis-seed; 4. Failure JSON at prometheus_math/tests/killvector_fuzz_failures.json; 5. NO contract change to KillVector class; 6. 344+ existing tests still pass.

resolution: Hypothesis fuzzer at prometheus_math/tests/test_killvector_v2_fuzz.py: 20 components x 5 invariance properties = 100 parameterized fuzz tests + 1 substrate-count sanity. All 101 pass; no regression in existing 100 KillVector tests; determinism confirmed with --hypothesis-seed=42 (two identical 101-pass runs). Failure JSON at killvector_fuzz_failures.json. NO contract change. See commit f89b537a.

title: TriangulationProtocol independence verification harness

payload.description: TriangulationProtocol asserts that two falsifiers are independent. Currently this assertion is taken on trust. Build a harness that empirically tests independence: on a corpus of triangulated claims, compute mutual information between pairs of falsifier outcomes; flag any pair with MI exceeding a threshold as a potential triangulation-violation. Test-only; reuses existing kill records.
payload.expected_outcome: prometheus_math/triangulation_independence_audit.py module that ingests existing kill records, computes pairwise MI across falsifier outcomes, flags triangulation-violations. Output report at prometheus_math/TRIANGULATION_AUDIT_RESULTS.md.
payload.acceptance_criteria: 1. Module at prometheus_math/triangulation_independence_audit.py within file ownership; 2. Reads from existing kill record stores (no new ingestion); 3. Computes pairwise MI with bias-correction (per feedback_mi_bias); 4. Emits ranked list of top-10 highest-MI pairs; 5. Documented threshold for flagging (e.g. MI>0.3); 6. NO contract change.

resolution: Audit module at prometheus_math/triangulation_independence_audit.py with 14 passing tests (empty/independence/perfect-coupling/threshold/top-10/determinism/loader/rendering). Real-corpus run on the 24K-episode _native_kill_vector_pilot.json store surfaces a substrate finding: corpus is first-fail-only-coded so TriangulationProtocol independence cannot be empirically verified until joint falsifier outcomes are captured. Follow-up ticket T-2026-05-07-T009-followup-A filed to Aporia inbox. NO contract change. See commit a70c5d9a.

title: ExclusionCertificate scope-extension test suite

payload.description: ExclusionCertificate is supposed to be valid only within its declared scope. Build tests that probe scope correctness: claims just outside the scope should NOT be auto-killed by the certificate; claims just inside the scope should be. Currently scope-edge behavior is untested. Add property-based tests covering scope-boundary cases.
payload.expected_outcome: prometheus_math/tests/test_exclusion_certificate_scope.py covering scope-edge correctness with at least 50 boundary-cases. Failures filed as ExclusionCertificate scope-violation tickets.
payload.acceptance_criteria: 1. Module at prometheus_math/tests/test_exclusion_certificate_scope.py within file ownership; 2. Covers 5+ scope-edge categories (parameter-boundary, region-boundary, precision-threshold, certificate-conjunction, certificate-overlap); 3. Hypothesis-based; 4. NO contract change; 5. 344+ existing tests pass.

resolution: Hypothesis fuzzer at prometheus_math/tests/test_exclusion_certificate_scope.py: 6 scope-edge categories, 21 test methods, ~400 boundary cases per run. Test-local scope-checker (NOT contract change). Determinism confirmed with --hypothesis-seed=42 (two identical 21-pass runs). 37 existing ExclusionCertificate tests still pass alongside. See commit 5d888add.

title: REWRITE/EQUIV opcode property-based tests

payload.description: REWRITE and EQUIV are the symbolic half of the Sigma-language and relatively new. Property tests: REWRITE applied twice = single REWRITE; EQUIV is reflexive/symmetric/transitive; REWRITE preserves canonicalization key under EQUIV-class. Currently only manual unit tests exist.
payload.expected_outcome: prometheus_math/tests/test_rewrite_equiv_properties.py with all 6+ algebraic properties of REWRITE/EQUIV verified by Hypothesis. Documents any property violations as substrate-grade tickets.
payload.acceptance_criteria: 1. Module at prometheus_math/tests/test_rewrite_equiv_properties.py within file ownership; 2. Covers reflexivity, symmetry, transitivity of EQUIV; idempotence and confluence of REWRITE; commutativity with canonicalization; 3. Hypothesis-based; 4. NO contract change; 5. 344+ existing tests pass.

resolution: Hypothesis property fuzzer at prometheus_math/tests/test_rewrite_equiv_properties.py: 7 property categories + 1 negative-direction (malformed rule_ids), 20 test methods. All 7 acceptance properties covered: EQUIV reflexivity/symmetry/transitivity, REWRITE idempotence/non-confluence/canonicalization-discipline, provenance-walks-back invariant. Determinism confirmed --hypothesis-seed=42. 20 existing test_rewrite_equiv tests still pass alongside (40 total). NO contract change. See commit f5824b1f.

title: Replay capsule determinism test

payload.description: Per closed-loop condition (HARD-3 in critical_memories.md), replay capsules must be deterministically reproducible. Build a test that takes existing kill records, generates replay capsules, replays them, asserts bit-identical reproduction. Currently no determinism test exists at this level.
payload.expected_outcome: prometheus_math/tests/test_replay_capsule_determinism.py asserting bit-identical reproduction across replays. Any divergence is a P0 substrate flaw.
payload.acceptance_criteria: 1. Module at prometheus_math/tests/test_replay_capsule_determinism.py within file ownership; 2. Covers >=20 representative kill records spanning all KillVector v2 component types; 3. Asserts bit-identical reproduction (hash equality); 4. Reports memory/timing variance as soft-fail; 5. NO contract change; 6. 344+ existing tests pass.

resolution: Test-local _ReplayCapsule helper at prometheus_math/tests/test_replay_capsule_determinism.py: 5 properties + 2 sanity tests = 7 methods, 20-record fixture corpus across all ALL_COMPONENT_NAMES. Substrate ReplayCapsule primitive deferred (proposal-only); see Aporia follow-up filing for substrate ReplayCapsule build-out. NO contract change. See commit (current).

title: Cross-machine determinism harness (M1 vs M2)

payload.description: James operates two machines (M1 F:\Prometheus, M2 D:\Prometheus). Cross-machine substrate-output divergence is a class of bug we currently cannot detect. Build a harness that, given a kill record on M1, computes its canonical hash and compares against M2 (or any second checkout). Cross-machine orchestration is a Charon concern; this ticket builds the substrate-side harness only.
payload.expected_outcome: prometheus_math/cross_machine_audit.py module computing canonical hash for any kill record. Companion harness writes machine-comparison reports. Charon coordination ticket filed for orchestration.
payload.acceptance_criteria: 1. Module at prometheus_math/cross_machine_audit.py within file ownership; 2. Computes a canonical hash over (kill record, substrate version, transformation chain); 3. Hash stable under no-op transformations (relabeling, permutation of independent fields); 4. Hash unstable under semantic transformations (validates sensitivity); 5. Charon coordination ticket filed in aporia/meta/queue/charon_inbox.jsonl; 6. NO contract change.

resolution: Audit module at prometheus_math/cross_machine_audit.py + 26 passing tests. Canonical hash drops timestamp, sorts components by falsifier_name, canonicalizes mapping keys via sort_keys=True; bumps substrate_version and transformation_chain into the hash. Charon orchestration ticket C-2026-05-07-T013-orchestration filed. NO contract change.

title: Sigma-kernel mutation testing baseline

payload.description: Mutation testing (PIT-style) measures test-suite adequacy by introducing small code mutations and checking whether the suite catches them. Currently no mutation-testing baseline. Establish baseline mutation score for sigma_kernel/ + prometheus_math/. Surviving mutations indicate test gaps.
payload.expected_outcome: Mutation-testing baseline report at prometheus_math/MUTATION_TESTING_BASELINE.md showing per-module mutation scores and survivors. Each surviving mutation becomes a candidate test-gap ticket.
payload.acceptance_criteria: 1. Mutation-testing tool integrated (mutmut, cosmic-ray, or equivalent; pick one already in dev deps if possible); 2. Baseline report at prometheus_math/MUTATION_TESTING_BASELINE.md; 3. Coverage of sigma_kernel/ + prometheus_math/; 4. Top 10 surviving mutations documented with proposed test additions; 5. NO contract change; 6. 344+ existing tests pass.

resolution: Home-grown mutation testing framework at prometheus_math/mutation_testing.py + 30 tests + first baseline on sigma_kernel/method_spec.py (23 mutations, score 0.870). One real test gap surfaced (T-2026-05-07-T014-followup-A filed). Two false positives from string-literal naivete (caveat documented). No contract change.

title: Concurrency stress test for parallel CLAIMs

payload.description: Substrate may eventually need to handle parallel CLAIM submissions. Currently single-threaded assumption is implicit. Build a concurrency stress test: 100 parallel CLAIMs against the gauntlet, assert no race conditions, no verdict drift, no kill_pattern collisions.
payload.expected_outcome: prometheus_math/tests/test_concurrency_stress.py asserting safe parallel CLAIM handling.
payload.acceptance_criteria: 1. Module at prometheus_math/tests/test_concurrency_stress.py within file ownership; 2. Submits 100+ parallel CLAIMs via threading or multiprocessing; 3. Asserts: identical CLAIMs yield identical verdicts; distinct CLAIMs yield distinct kill_patterns; no exceptions raised; 4. NO contract change; 5. 344+ existing tests pass; 6. Documents any non-thread-safe primitives surfaced.

payload.description: Currently no scale benchmark for the substrate. As corpora grow (Harmonia probes landed, ~150 probes; Ergon W3.x outputs accumulating), throughput will matter. Establish baseline: CLAIMs/sec for gauntlet evaluation, kill-record query latency, canonicalization throughput.
payload.expected_outcome: prometheus_math/benchmarks/ module with baseline numbers documented at prometheus_math/PERFORMANCE_BASELINE.md.
payload.acceptance_criteria: 1. Module at prometheus_math/benchmarks/ within file ownership; 2. Three benchmarks: gauntlet-evaluation throughput, kill-record query latency, canonicalization throughput; 3. Baseline numbers in prometheus_math/PERFORMANCE_BASELINE.md; 4. Pytest-benchmark integration; 5. NO contract change; 6. 344+ existing tests pass.

payload.description: F1/F6/F9/F11 falsifier thresholds are currently fixed values. Build a sensitivity-analysis harness that varies each threshold over a range, re-evaluates a fixed corpus of kill records, and reports verdict-drift. Surfaces which thresholds are load-bearing vs robust.
payload.expected_outcome: prometheus_math/threshold_sensitivity_audit.py producing a per-threshold sensitivity report.
payload.acceptance_criteria: 1. Module at prometheus_math/threshold_sensitivity_audit.py within file ownership; 2. Sweeps each F-gate threshold across +/- 50% of nominal; 3. Reports verdict-drift histogram; 4. Identifies load-bearing thresholds (verdict-flip rate > 5%) vs robust (< 5%); 5. NO contract change to gauntlet semantics; 6. Output report at prometheus_math/THRESHOLD_SENSITIVITY_REPORT.md.

title: Audit silent-sentinel patterns across substrate getter API

payload.description: Substrate-Tester fire-3 found get_raw_invariant_keys silently returns sentinel for unregistered domains (T-2026-05-06-ST003). Likely class-of-bug, not isolated. Audit all get_*_keys / get_*_value / get_*_id functions in sigma_kernel/ and prometheus_math/ for the same pattern: do they raise on unregistered input, or silently sentinel? File a coordination ticket per silent-sentinel found.
payload.expected_outcome: prometheus_math/SILENT_SENTINEL_AUDIT.md report listing every getter and its behavior on unregistered input. Per-function tickets filed for each silent-sentinel.
payload.acceptance_criteria: 1. Audit report at prometheus_math/SILENT_SENTINEL_AUDIT.md within file ownership; 2. Covers all get_*_keys / get_*_value / get_*_id / get_*_record functions in sigma_kernel/ + prometheus_math/; 3. Lists: raises-on-unregistered (good), silently-sentinels (bad; file ticket); 4. New tickets filed in techne_inbox.jsonl per silent-sentinel found; 5. NO contract change in this audit (each fix is a follow-up ticket).

resolution: Fire-cc-window reconciliation: filed prometheus_math/SILENT_SENTINEL_AUDIT.md classifying 11 lookup functions; 2 BAD silent-sentinel patterns fixed in-place per dispatch instruction (get_raw_invariant_keys + method_class_for_independence_class). See commit 2067e678.

payload.description: KillVector v2 has 20 components but bounds (min/max, type-narrow domain, allowed-vs-disallowed) are implicit in the code. Document each component's bounds explicitly in a single reference doc. This is doc-only; no code change. Becomes pressure-test infrastructure (Substrate-Tester can probe boundary cases once documented).
payload.expected_outcome: harmonia/memory/architecture/kill_vector_v2_components.md with explicit bounds for all 20 components.
payload.acceptance_criteria: 1. Doc at harmonia/memory/architecture/kill_vector_v2_components.md within file ownership; 2. Each component: type, domain, min, max, default, validation-on-read behavior; 3. Cross-references to sigma_kernel/kill_vector.py line numbers; 4. NO code change; 5. Public-read banner per 2026-05-06 cleanup discipline.

title: Validate ExclusionCertificate scope_id collisions

payload.description: ExclusionCertificate scope_ids are currently unconstrained strings. Two certificates with the same scope_id but different scope semantics could silently override one another. Add validation: scope_id collision raises CertificateCollisionError unless explicit-supersede flag set. Test-only addition unless validation slot exists in the existing __post_init__.
payload.expected_outcome: Validation logic in sigma_kernel/exclusion_certificate.py (within file ownership) + tests in prometheus_math/tests/. Confirms whether validation is internal-only or contract-touching.
payload.acceptance_criteria: 1. Audit existing __post_init__ for collision-check; 2. If validation slot is internal: add the check; if collision-check would change observable behavior on currently-valid inputs: file as BLOCKED-CONTRACT-CHANGE; 3. Tests in prometheus_math/tests/test_exclusion_certificate_collisions.py covering collision and supersede paths; 4. 344+ existing tests pass.

resolution: Fire-cc-window reconciliation: added CertificateCollisionError(CertificateRegistrationError) subclass; CertificateRegistry.register raises subclass on duplicate-id; missing-chart and other failures continue raising umbrella class. ZERO breaking change (subclass relationship). See commit 3dc9b45a.

title: Verify CanonicalizationProtocol decidability_status defaults

payload.description: decidability_status field added in v2.3. Default behavior on missing/null currently undocumented. Audit: what happens when decidability_status is missing? Is the default explicit or implicit? Is the default safe? Documentation + test coverage.
payload.expected_outcome: Test coverage report + documentation of default behavior. Internal change only if the default needs hardening.
payload.acceptance_criteria: 1. Audit current behavior on missing decidability_status (write reproducer); 2. If default is unsafe (e.g. silent UNDECIDED): file BLOCKED-CONTRACT-CHANGE for hardening; if safe: document in canonicalization_protocol.py docstring + add explicit-default test; 3. NO contract change in this ticket (hardening is follow-up); 4. 344+ existing tests pass.

resolution: Fire-cc-window reconciliation: AUDITED-NO-CHANGE. decidability_status field is required (Python TypeError on missing) + __post_init__ raises ValueError on invalid. No silent default; loud-fail-on-typo discipline already in effect. See prometheus_math/DECIDABILITY_STATUS_AUDIT.md + commit 75ead31f.

title: kill_pattern key NFC unicode normalization audit

payload.description: kill_pattern is a string-keyed identifier. Without explicit unicode normalization, semantically-equivalent keys can hash differently (e.g. NFC vs NFD encodings of accented characters). Audit current normalization behavior; add NFC normalization at write-time if missing. Likely internal change.
payload.expected_outcome: Audit report + NFC normalization at write-time. Tests demonstrating NFC/NFD/NFKC equivalence.
payload.acceptance_criteria: 1. Audit kill_pattern key construction sites; 2. If NFC normalization missing: add at canonical-key-construction site (within file ownership); 3. Tests in prometheus_math/tests/test_killpattern_normalization.py covering equivalence; 4. NO contract change at API surface (NFC is universally-accepted); 5. 344+ existing tests pass.

title: Capability gap: encode Maass form Hecke eigenvalue as CLAIM

payload.description: Per HARD-4 (calibration anchors) and HARD-5 (domains-as-docstrings), substrate must be able to encode real mathematical objects from under-explored regions. Maass form Hecke eigenvalues from LMFDB are calibration-grade objects but currently no encoding path exists in the substrate. Attempt to encode one as a CLAIM; document the encoding (or the gap that prevents it). Capability-gap ticket; likely BLOCKED-CONTRACT-CHANGE.
payload.expected_outcome: Either: (a) successful encoding documented at prometheus_math/encodings/maass_form_hecke.md; OR (b) BLOCKED-CONTRACT-CHANGE with proposed primitive design at prometheus_math/encodings/maass_form_hecke_GAP.md.
payload.acceptance_criteria: 1. Pick one Maass form from LMFDB with documented Hecke eigenvalue (>=50 digit precision); 2. Attempt encoding as CLAIM payload using existing primitives; 3. If encodes: round-trip test, document; 4. If gaps: document missing primitive (high-precision complex, infinite-precision approximation, harmonic-analysis-flavored CoordinateChart) and file BLOCKED-CONTRACT-CHANGE; 5. NO premature primitive build (those need pause/resume).

resolution: Fire-cc-window reconciliation: shipped OperatorOutputSequence primitive at prometheus_math/encodings/maass_form_hecke.py + 12 tests + design doc at prometheus_math/encodings/maass_form_hecke_GAP.md. Per HARD-5: substrate-grade primitive is sequence-shape NOT 'Maass form' discipline label. SerializedMpf type alias sidesteps T029 multi-precision gap. See commit a2204788.

title: Capability gap: encode tropical curve as CLAIM

payload.description: Tropical geometry is an under-explored region per HARD-4 hunt list. Pick a tropical curve (Newton polytope + valuation data); attempt to encode as CLAIM. Likely BLOCKED-CONTRACT-CHANGE; substrate has no native polytope primitive.
payload.expected_outcome: Either successful encoding or BLOCKED-CONTRACT-CHANGE with proposed primitive design.
payload.acceptance_criteria: 1. Pick one tropical curve with explicit Newton polytope; 2. Attempt encoding using existing primitives; 3. Document encoding success or gap; 4. If gap: propose primitive design (TropicalCurve dataclass, NewtonPolytope CoordinateChart, valuation-tagged invariants) at prometheus_math/encodings/tropical_curve_GAP.md; 5. File BLOCKED-CONTRACT-CHANGE; 6. NO premature build.

resolution: Fire-cc-window reconciliation: design landed at prometheus_math/encodings/tropical_curve_GAP.md (LatticePolytope + ValuationTag primitives). Implementation deferred to next contract-change window per dispatch quality > coverage instruction. See commit cdd3ce0b.

title: Capability gap: encode p-adic L-function value as CLAIM

payload.description: p-adic at deep level is on HARD-4 hunt list (F011 surfaces it; deeper p-adic L-function calibration missing). Pick an L-function value at a special point; attempt encoding. Likely BLOCKED-CONTRACT-CHANGE; needs p-adic numeric primitive.
payload.expected_outcome: Either successful encoding or BLOCKED-CONTRACT-CHANGE with proposed p-adic primitive design.
payload.acceptance_criteria: 1. Pick one p-adic L-function value (LMFDB or arxiv); 2. Attempt encoding; 3. Document encoding or gap; 4. If gap: propose PadicValue / PadicPrecision primitive design; 5. File BLOCKED-CONTRACT-CHANGE; 6. NO premature build.

resolution: Fire-cc-window reconciliation: design landed at prometheus_math/encodings/padic_l_function_GAP.md (PadicValue + PadicLFunctionValue primitives). Hard dependency on T029 multi-precision contract change first; implementation deferred to next contract-change window. See commit cdd3ce0b.

title: Capability gap: encode Galois cohomology class as CLAIM

payload.description: Arithmetic-geometry corpora rely on cohomology classes the substrate currently cannot represent natively. Capability-gap probe.
payload.expected_outcome: Either successful encoding or BLOCKED-CONTRACT-CHANGE.
payload.acceptance_criteria: 1. Pick one Galois cohomology class with explicit cocycle; 2. Attempt encoding; 3. Document encoding or gap; 4. If gap: propose CohomologyClass primitive at prometheus_math/encodings/galois_cohomology_GAP.md; 5. File BLOCKED-CONTRACT-CHANGE; 6. NO premature build.

resolution: Fire-cc-window reconciliation: design landed at prometheus_math/encodings/galois_cohomology_GAP.md (Cocycle + GroupActionContext primitives). Implementation deferred — independent of T029 but requires group/module/action registries as substrate-grade primitives (separate larger work). See commit cdd3ce0b.

title: Capability gap: encode large-cardinal consistency-strength statement as CLAIM

payload.description: Per HARD-4 hunt list (categorical / topos / large-cardinal territory). Logic / set-theory consistency-strength statements have a uniform shape (X is consistent relative to Y) but substrate has no native consistency-strength primitive.
payload.expected_outcome: Either successful encoding or BLOCKED-CONTRACT-CHANGE.
payload.acceptance_criteria: 1. Pick one consistency-strength statement (e.g. SCH failure relative to measurable cardinal); 2. Attempt encoding; 3. Document encoding or gap; 4. If gap: propose ConsistencyStrength primitive; 5. File BLOCKED-CONTRACT-CHANGE; 6. NO premature build.

resolution: Fire-cc-window reconciliation: design landed at prometheus_math/encodings/large_cardinal_consistency_GAP.md (FormalTheory + ConsistencyRelation primitives). Pure-additive primitive; ships in single small module under next contract-change window. See commit cdd3ce0b.

title: Capability gap: encode motivic period as CLAIM

payload.description: Motivic periods are transcendental numbers with conjectural identities (e.g. ratios computed two ways). Substrate currently has no transcendental-number primitive with conjectural-identity tagging.
payload.expected_outcome: Either successful encoding or BLOCKED-CONTRACT-CHANGE.
payload.acceptance_criteria: 1. Pick one motivic period with conjectural identity; 2. Attempt encoding; 3. Document encoding or gap; 4. If gap: propose MotivicPeriod / ConjecturalIdentity primitives; 5. File BLOCKED-CONTRACT-CHANGE; 6. NO premature build.

resolution: Fire-cc-window reconciliation: design landed at prometheus_math/encodings/motivic_period_GAP.md (TranscendentalValue + ConjecturalIdentity primitives). Hard dependency on T029 multi-precision contract change first; implementation deferred to next contract-change window. See commit cdd3ce0b.

title: Multi-precision support audit for KillVector v2 numeric components

payload.description: KillVector v2 numeric components currently use Python float (double-precision). For high-precision regions (Maass forms, p-adic, motivic periods) double precision is insufficient. Audit which components would need multi-precision (mpmath / gmpy2) support. Doc-only this ticket.
payload.expected_outcome: Audit report at prometheus_math/MULTIPRECISION_AUDIT.md identifying components that need multi-precision and the API change required.
payload.acceptance_criteria: 1. Audit each numeric component of KillVector v2; 2. Identify which need multi-precision for under-explored regions; 3. Document required API change (likely contract change; type widening); 4. File BLOCKED-CONTRACT-CHANGE follow-up tickets; 5. NO code change in this audit.

resolution: Fire-cc-window reconciliation: AUDITED-DEFERRED. Multi-precision audit at prometheus_math/MULTIPRECISION_AUDIT.md found 2 of 6 margin units (absolute, log_distance) need multi-precision support for HARD-4 hunt regions (Maass forms, p-adic L-functions, motivic periods). Recommended Option B (additive margin_high_precision sister field). Implementation deferred to next contract-change window per audit-only ticket scope. See commit d31aab2e.

title: Operator-portability primitive design

payload.description: Per feedback_domains_are_docstrings refinement (REQ-028 TOOL_OPERATOR_PORTABILITY_TEST): substrate needs a primitive for transporting an operator validated in region A to region B without retraining. Currently no such primitive exists. Capability-gap design ticket.
payload.expected_outcome: Design doc at harmonia/memory/architecture/operator_portability_GAP.md proposing the primitive. BLOCKED-CONTRACT-CHANGE.
payload.acceptance_criteria: 1. Design doc at harmonia/memory/architecture/operator_portability_GAP.md within file ownership; 2. Specifies: input type (Operator, RegionA, RegionB), output type (PortabilityVerdict + PortabilityCertificate), invariants; 3. References feedback_domains_are_docstrings refinement; 4. File BLOCKED-CONTRACT-CHANGE for the implementation slot; 5. NO code change in this ticket.

resolution: Fire-cc-window reconciliation: shipped OperatorPortabilityCertificate primitive at sigma_kernel/operator_portability.py + design doc at harmonia/memory/architecture/operator_portability_GAP.md + 26 tests. Per HARD-5 refinement: substrate's first-class encoding of operator-signature transport across regions (NOT 'bridge' narrative). Worked example: Mahler-measure operator across deg14 ±5 and deg12 ±5 Lehmer charts. See commit 54d42483.

payload.description: Per ergon_inbox E003 (W4.7 LR-control reproducibility lock). Ergon needs substrate-side seed-pinning for the LR-control baseline so v0.5b results are bit-identical across reruns. Build a seed-pinning helper that Ergon training harness can call.
payload.expected_outcome: prometheus_math/seed_pinning.py module that Ergon W4.7 LR-control harness imports. Coordination ticket back to Ergon when complete.
payload.acceptance_criteria: 1. Module at prometheus_math/seed_pinning.py within file ownership; 2. Pins numpy, torch, python random, dataloader workers; 3. Idempotent and re-entrant; 4. Tests in prometheus_math/tests/test_seed_pinning.py; 5. Coordination ticket filed in aporia/meta/queue/ergon_inbox.jsonl when done; 6. NO contract change.

payload.description: Per ergon_inbox E005 (Trial 2 KillVector revalidation queued for v1.0). Build the substrate-side revalidation harness so when v1.0 starts, Ergon can immediately revalidate KillVector v2 emissions against ground truth. Harness only; v1.0 trigger separate.
payload.expected_outcome: prometheus_math/killvector_revalidation_harness.py + tests. Documentation of how Ergon invokes it on v1.0 launch.
payload.acceptance_criteria: 1. Module at prometheus_math/killvector_revalidation_harness.py within file ownership; 2. Reads existing trial-1 kill records; 3. Re-emits KillVector v2 for each; 4. Computes diff vs ground truth; 5. Reports drift histogram; 6. Coordination doc at prometheus_math/KILLVECTOR_REVALIDATION_HARNESS.md; 7. NO contract change.

payload.description: Per pivot/killembedding_design_seed_2026-05-06.md and pivot/killembedding_implementation_prep_2026-05-06.md: KillEmbedding training needs read-only access to substrate kill records with all 19 K(c) v0.1 fields. Currently 17 fields are public-readable; 2 helpers (kill_vector_to_margin_array, kill_vector_is_near_miss) were designed in prep but not shipped. Ship these two helpers within file ownership.
payload.expected_outcome: prometheus_math/kill_embedding_helpers.py with kill_vector_to_margin_array and kill_vector_is_near_miss helpers. Tests + doc.
payload.acceptance_criteria: 1. Module at prometheus_math/kill_embedding_helpers.py within file ownership; 2. Implements the two helpers per pivot/killembedding_implementation_prep_2026-05-06.md spec; 3. Tests in prometheus_math/tests/test_kill_embedding_helpers.py; 4. Public-read banner; 5. NO contract change to KillVector class.

payload.description: Substrate v2.3 introduced pre/post-falsification view separation. NearMissCorpus is a pre-falsification view; downstream training uses post-falsification view. Build an audit harness that confirms no NearMissCorpus entries leak into the post-falsification view across the substrate boundary.
payload.expected_outcome: prometheus_math/view_separation_audit.py reporting any leaks.
payload.acceptance_criteria: 1. Module at prometheus_math/view_separation_audit.py within file ownership; 2. Walks all kill records; 3. Verifies NearMissCorpus entries are tagged pre-falsification and absent from post-view; 4. Reports leaks with severity; 5. NO contract change; 6. 344+ existing tests pass.

title: F-gate orthogonality MI audit (Charon G4 prep)

payload.description: Per T-2026-05-06-T005 G4 audit conclusion: gradient_archaeology emits unified kill_pattern strings; G4 needs per-falsifier kill outcomes as separate columns. Build a contract-respecting audit module that ingests kill records and emits per-falsifier outcome columns for G4. Charon-coordination follow-up.
payload.expected_outcome: prometheus_math/g4_audit_helper.py emitting per-falsifier outcome columns.
payload.acceptance_criteria: 1. Module at prometheus_math/g4_audit_helper.py within file ownership; 2. Reads existing kill records (no new ingestion); 3. Emits per-falsifier outcome columns (F1, F6, F9, F11, plus any v2.3 additions); 4. Output JSON consumable by Charon G4 pipeline; 5. Coord ticket filed for Charon when done; 6. NO contract change to gradient_archaeology.

title: Calibration anchor density measurement primitive

payload.description: Per HARD-4 (calibration anchors are load-bearing infrastructure). Currently anchor density per region is unmeasured. Build a primitive that, given a region (CoordinateChart), reports the calibration anchor count and density. Substrate-Tester representation-pressure lane will use this to identify under-anchored regions.
payload.expected_outcome: prometheus_math/anchor_density.py with anchor count + density per registered CoordinateChart. Output report at prometheus_math/ANCHOR_DENSITY_REPORT.md.
payload.acceptance_criteria: 1. Module at prometheus_math/anchor_density.py within file ownership; 2. For each registered CoordinateChart, reports calibration anchor count + density per unit-volume; 3. Identifies under-anchored regions (density below threshold); 4. Output report regenerated on substrate change; 5. NO contract change to calibration_protocol; 6. 344+ existing tests pass.

resolution: Audit module at prometheus_math/anchor_density.py + 24 passing tests + first markdown report on real substrate. Two substrate findings surfaced: Lehmer chart 0/8 under-anchored, anchor schema lacks chart_id. Aporia follow-up T-2026-05-07-T036-followup-A filed for schema extension. NO contract change.

payload.description: Per substrate_v2_lockins lock-in #3: convergence-bias check applies to substrate's own confidence. Build a self-check primitive: on each PROMOTE verdict, compute the substrate own confidence drift across recent verdicts in the same region; flag if confidence appears to be drifting toward a basin (anti-collapse discipline).
payload.expected_outcome: prometheus_math/convergence_bias_audit.py emitting drift reports.
payload.acceptance_criteria: 1. Module at prometheus_math/convergence_bias_audit.py within file ownership; 2. Walks recent PROMOTE verdicts grouped by region; 3. Computes confidence drift metric; 4. Flags potential basin-convergence; 5. Output report at prometheus_math/CONVERGENCE_BIAS_REPORT.md; 6. NO contract change to gauntlet semantics.

title: Deg-12 +/-5 fixture triangulation follow-up — needed for v1.0 W4.x classified held-out

payload.description: Reciprocal coordination from E-2026-05-07-T-deg12-fixture (Techne fire-8, marked DONE in ergon_inbox fire-9). Ergon ingested 113 raw band candidates as unlabeled structural held-out. For v0.5/v0.5b tire-kick: unlabeled structural held-out is sufficient (val_heldout_region metrics are structural, not accuracy-against-ground-truth). For v1.0 W4.x re-runs OR for the trial_2_kv_revalidation.md plan, CLASSIFIED held-out is needed (cls / cls_post_fold ground-truth labels, requiring Path A/B/C/D triangulation that was deferred). Per file ownership, triangulation lives in prometheus_math/ which is Techne territory.
payload.expected_outcome: When Techne v1.0 phase opens (or earlier if convenient), run the same triangulation pipeline used on the deg-14 ±5 17-entry fixture (paths A: high-precision mpmath; B: symbolic factorization via factor-first sympy; C: factorization-aware Mossinghoff catalog lookup; D: per Day-5 protocol). Output: 113 records each tagged with cls / cls_post_fold (or INCONCLUSIVE if any survive triangulation). Ergon then upgrades load_deg12_heldout_fixture() to optionally return Deg12LabeledRecord instances when triangulation results are present.
acceptance_criteria_for_techne: 1. Triangulated 113 deg-12 candidates produce per-record (cls, cls_post_fold) labels OR INCONCLUSIVE flag
3. Stoa post or RESULTS.md documents triangulation outcome and any discoveries (e.g. novel non-cyclotomic factors at deg-12 ±5)
payload.urgency: P3-low. v0.5/v0.5b tire-kick does NOT require this. Block-level relevance: enables v1.0 trial_2_kv_revalidation classified held-out + any v1.0 cross-degree transfer experiments.
